question: Definitive host is one

options.text: In which sexual multiplication takes place and harbors adult form
options.feedback: Correct! The definitive host is where the parasite reaches sexual maturity and reproduces sexually.

options.text: In which asexual multiplication takes place and harbors adult form
options.feedback: Incorrect. This describes the intermediate host, not the definitive host.

options.text: In which sexual multiplication takes place and harbors larval form
options.feedback: Incorrect. The definitive host harbors the adult form, not the larval form.

options.text: In which asexual multiplication takes place and harbors adult form
options.feedback: Incorrect. This describes the intermediate host, not the definitive host.

question: Autoinfection is seen in all except

options.feedback: Incorrect. Hymenolepis nana can cause autoinfection.
Incorrect. Enterobius vermicularis (pinworm) can cause autoinfection through the anal-oral route.
Incorrect. Taenia solium can cause autoinfection leading to cysticercosis.
Correct! Ascaris lumbricoides does NOT exhibit autoinfection.

question: Antigenic variation is exhibited by

options.feedback: Incorrect. Entamoeba does not exhibit antigenic variation.
Incorrect. Schistosoma does not exhibit antigenic variation.
Correct! Trypanosoma exhibits antigenic variation to evade the host immune response.
Incorrect. Leishmania does not exhibit antigenic variation.

question: Which parasite enters the body by piercing the skin

options.feedback: Incorrect. Trichuris is ingested as embryonated eggs.
Incorrect. Ascaris is ingested as embryonated eggs.
Correct! Necator americanus (hookworm) enters by penetrating the skin.
Incorrect. Plasmodium enters through the bite of an infected mosquito.

question: Which parasitic infection leads to malignancy

options.feedback: Incorrect. Babesiosis is not associated with malignancy.
Correct! Clonorchis sinensis infection is associated with cholangiocarcinoma.
Incorrect. T. cruzi causes Chagas disease, not malignancy.
Incorrect. While S. hematobium is associated with bladder cancer, the correct answer for this question is Clonorchis sinensis.

question: Xenodiagnosis is useful in

options.feedback: Incorrect. Xenodiagnosis is not typically used for W. bancrofti.
Incorrect. While xenodiagnosis was historically used for T. cruzi, the answer is 'All of the above'.
Incorrect. Xenodiagnosis is not typically used for T. spiralis.
Correct! Xenodiagnosis is useful for all of the listed parasites.

question: The following are zoonotic disease except

options.feedback: Incorrect. Leishmaniasis is a zoonotic disease.
Incorrect. Balantidiasis is a zoonotic disease (pigs are reservoir).
Correct! Scabies is transmitted human-to-human and is NOT zoonotic.
Incorrect. Taeniasis is a zoonotic disease (cattle/pigs are intermediate hosts).

question: Two hosts are required in

options.feedback: Correct! Taenia solium requires two hosts: pig (intermediate) and human (definitive).
Incorrect. E. histolytica requires only one host (human).
Incorrect. T. trichiura requires only one host (human).
Incorrect. Giardia requires only one host (human).

question: Which of the following parasite passes its life cycle through three hosts

options.feedback: Incorrect. F. hepatica requires two hosts (snail and mammal).
Incorrect. F. buski requires two hosts (snail and mammal).
Incorrect. Schistosoma requires one intermediate host (snail) and one definitive host (human).
Correct! C. sinensis requires three hosts: snail → fish → human.

question: Man is the intermediate host for

options.feedback: Incorrect. Man is the definitive host for S. stercoralis.
Correct! Man is the intermediate host for P. vivax (sexual cycle occurs in mosquito).
Incorrect. Man is the definitive host for E. histolytica.
Incorrect. Man is the definitive host for E. vermicularis.

question: Protozoa belong to kingdom

options.feedback: Incorrect. Monera includes bacteria and cyanobacteria.
Correct! Protozoa belong to Kingdom Protista.
Incorrect. Plantae includes plants.
Incorrect. While some classifications place protozoa in Animalia, the modern classification places them in Protista.

question: All are intercellular parasites except

options.feedback: Incorrect. Leishmania is an intracellular parasite.
Incorrect. Plasmodium is an intracellular parasite.
Incorrect. Toxoplasma is an intracellular parasite.
Correct! All of the listed parasites are intracellular, so 'None of the above' is the answer.

question: Non-nuclear DNA present in addition to nucleus in protozoan parasite is

options.feedback: Incorrect. Chromatid bodies are glycogen-containing structures in Entamoeba cysts.
Incorrect. Karyosome is the nucleolus-like structure in the nucleus.
Correct! Kinetoplast is the non-nuclear DNA found in hemoflagellates.
Incorrect. Basal bodies are associated with flagella.

question: Entamoeba histolytica trophozoites multiply by

options.feedback: Correct! E. histolytica trophozoites multiply by binary fission.
Incorrect. Schizogony is seen in Plasmodium.
Incorrect. Gametogony is sexual reproduction seen in Plasmodium.
Incorrect. Only binary fission applies to E. histolytica.

question: In humans, malarial parasites multiply by

options.feedback: Incorrect. Binary fission is not the primary mode in malaria.
Incorrect. Budding is seen in yeast, not malaria.
Incorrect. Gametogony occurs in the mosquito vector.
Correct! Plasmodium multiplies by schizogony (asexual reproduction) in humans.

question: Which of the following is not a flagellate

options.feedback: Correct! Naegleria is an amoeboflagellate (has both forms) but is primarily classified as an amoeba.
Incorrect. Leishmania is a flagellate (promastigote form).
Incorrect. Giardia is a flagellate.
Incorrect. Dientamoeba is a flagellate (though it lacks a flagellum in its stage).

question: The main reservoir of Entamoeba histolytica is

options.feedback: Correct! Man is the main reservoir of E. histolytica.
Incorrect. Dirty water is a transmission vehicle, not a reservoir.
Incorrect. Dogs are not the main reservoir.
Incorrect. Monkeys are not the main reservoir.

question: The infective form of Entamoeba histolytica is

options.feedback: Incorrect. Trophozoites are the invasive form but are not infective (they die outside the body).
Incorrect. Binucleate cysts are immature cysts.
Correct! The mature quadrinucleate cyst (4 nuclei) is the infective form.
Incorrect. The quadrinucleate cyst is the infective form.

question: The pathogenicity of Entamoeba histolytica is indicated by

options.feedback: Correct! Zymodeme pattern indicates the pathogenicity of E. histolytica.
Incorrect. Size alone does not indicate pathogenicity.
Incorrect. Nuclear pattern alone does not indicate pathogenicity.
Incorrect. ELISA detects antibodies, not pathogenicity.

question: Most common site for extraintestinal amoebiasis is

options.feedback: Correct! The liver is the most common site for extraintestinal amoebiasis.
Incorrect. The lung is the second most common site.
Incorrect. Brain involvement is rare.
Incorrect. Spleen involvement is rare.

question: Amoebic liver abscess can be diagnosed by demonstrating

options.feedback: Incorrect. Cysts are not found in liver abscess pus.
Correct! Trophozoites in the pus confirm amoebic liver abscess.
Incorrect. This would indicate intestinal infection, not liver abscess.
Incorrect. Trophozoites in feces indicate intestinal infection.

question: Stool of amoebic dysentery has all of the following characteristics except

options.feedback: Incorrect. Charcot-Leyden crystals are seen in amoebic dysentery.
Incorrect. Pyknotic bodies are seen in amoebic dysentery.
Incorrect. RBCs are seen in amoebic dysentery (bloody stool).
Correct! Ghost cells are NOT characteristic of amoebic dysentery.

question: The term amoeboma is used to describe

options.feedback: Incorrect. Amoeboma is not the same as liver abscess.

options.text: Skin lesion due to draining amoebic abscess
options.feedback: Incorrect. This describes a different manifestation.
Correct! Amoeboma is a granulomatous mass at the ileocaecal junction.
Incorrect. Amoeboma refers to the granuloma at ileocaecal junction.

question: True statement regarding Entamoeba histolytica is

options.text: The trophozoites are infective to man
options.feedback: Incorrect. Trophozoites are not infective (they die outside the body).
Incorrect. The mature cyst has 4 nuclei, not an eccentric nucleolus.

options.text: It can cause primary amoebic encephalitis
options.feedback: Incorrect. Primary amoebic encephalitis is caused by Naegleria or Acanthamoeba.

options.text: Cysts are resistant to chlorine concentration used in drinking water
options.feedback: Correct! E. histolytica cysts are resistant to chlorine levels used in drinking water.

question: All are nonpathogenic amoeba living in the lumen of large intestine except

options.feedback: Incorrect. E. coli is a nonpathogenic commensal in the large intestine.
Incorrect. E. hartmanni is a nonpathogenic commensal in the large intestine.
Incorrect. E. nana is a nonpathogenic commensal in the large intestine.
Correct! E. gingivalis is found in the oral cavity, NOT the large intestine.

question: Chronic amoebic keratitis is seen in

options.feedback: Incorrect. E. histolytica does not cause keratitis.
Correct! Acanthamoeba causes chronic amoebic keratitis.
Incorrect. N. fowleri causes meningoencephalitis, not keratitis.
Incorrect. Hemoflagellates do not cause keratitis.

question: Etiologic agent of granulomatous amoebic encephalitis is

options.feedback: Incorrect. E. histolytica does not cause granulomatous encephalitis.
Correct! Acanthamoeba causes granulomatous amoebic encephalitis.
Incorrect. Naegleria causes primary amoebic meningoencephalitis.
Incorrect. D. fragilis is a nonpathogenic commensal.

question: Normal habitat of Giardia is

options.feedback: Correct! Giardia inhabits the duodenum and jejunum.
Incorrect. Giardia does not inhabit the stomach.
Incorrect. Giardia does not inhabit the caecum.
Incorrect. Giardia does not inhabit the ileum.

question: All of the following protozoans are found in small intestine except

options.feedback: Incorrect. Giardia is found in the small intestine.
Correct! Balantidium coli is found in the large intestine, NOT the small intestine.
Incorrect. Cyclospora is found in the small intestine.
Incorrect. Isospora is found in the small intestine.

question: The following is true of giardiasis except

options.text: Fever and presence of blood and mucus in stool
options.feedback: Correct! Giardiasis does NOT cause fever or blood/mucus in stool; it causes watery diarrhea.
Incorrect. This is true of giardiasis.

options.text: Duodenum and jejunum are the prime sites of involvement
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is true of giardiasis.

options.text: Giardia cysts are resistant to desiccation
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is true of giardiasis.

question: Giardia lamblia was discovered by

options.feedback: Incorrect. The parasite was named after Giard, but discovered by Leeuwenhoek.
Incorrect. Hooke is known for cell discovery.
Correct! Leeuwenhoek discovered Giardia lamblia.
Incorrect. Losch discovered Entamoeba histolytica.

question: Drug of choice in giardiasis is

options.feedback: Correct! Metronidazole is the drug of choice for giardiasis.
Incorrect. Albendazole is used for helminth infections.
Incorrect. Thiabendazole is used for Strongyloides.
Incorrect. Diloxanide furoate is used for asymptomatic amoebiasis.

question: True about Giardia is

options.feedback: Correct! Giardia is a common cause of traveler's diarrhea.
Incorrect. Giardia inhabits the duodenum and jejunum.
Incorrect. Cysts are the infective form.

options.text: Encystment of trophozoites occur in jejunum
options.feedback: Incorrect. Encystment occurs in the colon.

question: Which one of the following tests is used for diagnosis of Giardia lamblia infections

options.feedback: Correct! Enterotest (string test) is used to diagnose Giardia.
Incorrect. Casoni's test is for hydatid disease.
Incorrect. Parasight F test is for malaria.
Incorrect. Napier's test is for kala-azar.

question: Motility of Trichomonas vaginalis is described as

options.feedback: Incorrect. Amoeboid motility is seen in Entamoeba.
Correct! T. vaginalis motility is described as jerky.
Incorrect. Falling leaf motility is seen in Giardia.
Incorrect. Lashing motility is seen in trypanosomes.

question: Vaginal discharge in Trichomonas vaginitis is

options.feedback: Incorrect. The discharge is not colorless.
Correct! T. vaginalis causes yellow/greenish, frothy discharge.
Incorrect. Curd-white discharge is seen in candidiasis.
Incorrect. Blood-stained discharge is not typical.

question: All of the following protozoan can be transmitted by sexual contact except

options.feedback: Incorrect. T. vaginalis is transmitted sexually.
Incorrect. E. histolytica can be transmitted sexually (oral-anal contact).
Correct! E. hominis is NOT transmitted by sexual contact.
Incorrect. Giardia can be transmitted sexually (oral-anal contact).

question: Vector for Trypanosoma cruzi is

options.feedback: Correct! Reduvid bug (kissing bug) is the vector for T. cruzi.
Incorrect. Tsetse fly is the vector for T. brucei.
Incorrect. Sandfly is the vector for Leishmania.
Incorrect. Hard tick is the vector for Babesia.

question: All of the following are obligate intracellular parasite except

options.feedback: Incorrect. Plasmodium is an obligate intracellular parasite.
Incorrect. T. cruzi is an obligate intracellular parasite.
Incorrect. T. gondii is an obligate intracellular parasite.
Correct! T. brucei gambiense is extracellular (NOT obligate intracellular).

question: Romana's sign occurs in

options.feedback: Incorrect. Romana's sign is not seen in babesiosis.
Incorrect. Romana's sign is not seen in leishmaniasis.
Correct! Romana's sign is seen in Chagas disease (American trypanosomiasis).
Incorrect. Romana's sign is not seen in schistosomiasis.

question: Vector for T. brucei gambiense is

options.feedback: Incorrect. Sandfly is the vector for Leishmania.
Incorrect. Reduvid bug is the vector for T. cruzi.
Correct! Tsetse fly is the vector for T. brucei gambiense.
Incorrect. House fly is not a vector for trypanosomes.

question: Winterbottom sign in sleeping sickness refers to

options.feedback: Incorrect. This is not Winterbottom sign.
Correct! Winterbottom sign refers to posterior cervical lymphadenitis.
Incorrect. Narcolepsy is a symptom, not Winterbottom sign.
Incorrect. This is not Winterbottom sign.

question: The drug that can clear trypanosomes from blood and lymph nodes and is active in late nervous system stages of African sleeping sickness is

options.feedback: Incorrect. Emetine is used for amoebiasis.
Correct! Melarsoprol is active in late nervous system stages of African sleeping sickness.
Incorrect. Nifurtimox is used for Chagas disease.
Incorrect. Suramin is used for early stages of African sleeping sickness.

question: Which of the following is not true about West African trypanosomiasis

options.feedback: Incorrect. This is true - humans are the primary reservoir.
Incorrect. This is true - T. gambiense causes low parasitemia.
Incorrect. This is true - West African trypanosomiasis is chronic.
Correct! West African trypanosomiasis causes PROMINENT lymphadenopathy (Winterbottom sign), not minimal.

question: Chronic infections with which of the following hemoflagellates may be associated with megaesophagus or megacolon

options.feedback: Incorrect. T. gambiense does not cause megaesophagus or megacolon.
Correct! T. cruzi can cause megaesophagus and megacolon (Chagas disease).
Incorrect. L. donovani causes kala-azar, not megaesophagus.
Incorrect. L. tropica causes cutaneous leishmaniasis.

question: True about visceral leishmaniasis is/are

options.feedback: Incorrect. Visceral leishmaniasis is caused by L. donovani.

options.text: Post leishmaniasis dermatitis develops in 20% of patients
options.feedback: Incorrect. Post-kala-azar dermal leishmaniasis develops in about 10-20% of patients.
Correct! Antimonial compounds (sodium stibogluconate) are used for treatment.
Incorrect. The vector is sandfly, not tsetse fly.

question: Which of the following is most severely affected in Kala-azar

options.feedback: Correct! The spleen is most severely affected in kala-azar (splenomegaly).
Incorrect. The liver is affected but not as severely as the spleen.
Incorrect. Lymph nodes are affected but not the most severely.
Incorrect. Bone marrow is affected but not the most severely.

question: LD bodies are

options.text: Amastigotes of Leishmania donovani inside RBCs
options.feedback: Incorrect. LD bodies are inside macrophages, not RBCs.
Incorrect. LD bodies are not giant cells.
Incorrect. LD bodies are not degenerative lesions.

options.text: Amastigotes of Leishmania donovani inside macrophages
options.feedback: Correct! LD bodies are amastigotes of L. donovani inside macrophages.

question: In a case of Kala-azar, aldehyde test becomes positive after

options.feedback: Incorrect. The aldehyde test becomes positive after a longer duration.
Incorrect. The aldehyde test becomes positive after a longer duration.
Incorrect. The aldehyde test becomes positive after 12 weeks.
Correct! Aldehyde test becomes positive after 12 weeks of infection.

question: Mucocutaneous leishmaniasis is caused by

options.feedback: Correct! L. braziliensis causes mucocutaneous leishmaniasis.
Incorrect. L. donovani causes visceral leishmaniasis.
Incorrect. L. tropica causes cutaneous leishmaniasis.
Incorrect. L. braziliensis is the correct answer.

question: Chiclero's ulcer is caused by

options.feedback: Correct! Chiclero's ulcer is caused by L. mexicana complex.
Incorrect. L. braziliensis causes mucocutaneous leishmaniasis.
Incorrect. L. tropica causes cutaneous leishmaniasis.
Incorrect. L. infantum causes visceral leishmaniasis.

question: Old RBCs are preferentially infected by

options.feedback: Incorrect. P. falciparum infects young RBCs.
Correct! P. malariae preferentially infects old RBCs.
Incorrect. P. vivax infects young RBCs (reticulocytes).
Incorrect. P. ovale infects young RBCs.

question: The infective form of the malaria parasite is

options.feedback: Incorrect. Oocyst is a stage in the mosquito, not infective to humans.
Correct! Sporozoites are the infective form injected by mosquitoes.
Incorrect. Bradyzoites are found in Toxoplasma.
Incorrect. Tachyzoites are found in Toxoplasma.

question: Prolonged parasitism in malaria is due to

options.feedback: Incorrect. Prolonged parasitism is not primarily due to antigenic variation.
Correct! The intracellular location of the parasite helps it evade the immune system.
Incorrect. Immunosuppression is not the primary mechanism.
Incorrect. Sequestration is specific to P. falciparum.

question: Malaria pigment is formed by

options.feedback: Incorrect. The parasite does not form the pigment directly.
Incorrect. Bilirubin is not the malaria pigment.
Correct! Malaria pigment (hemozoin) is formed from hemoglobin breakdown.
Incorrect. Only hemoglobin is correct.

question: Schuffner's dot in RBCs are seen in infection with

options.feedback: Correct! Schuffner's dots are seen in P. vivax and P. ovale infections.
Incorrect. P. falciparum shows Maurer's dots.
Incorrect. P. malariae shows Ziemann's dots.
Incorrect. While P. ovale also shows Schuffner's dots, P. vivax is the classic answer.

question: Quartan Malaria is caused by

options.feedback: Incorrect. P. vivax causes tertian malaria.
Incorrect. P. falciparum causes malignant tertian malaria.
Correct! P. malariae causes quartan malaria (every 72 hours).
Incorrect. P. ovale causes tertian malaria.

question: Schizonts of Plasmodium falciparum are not found in peripheral blood because

options.text: Schizonts are absent in the life cycle
options.feedback: Incorrect. Schizonts are present in the life cycle.
Incorrect. This is not the reason.

options.text: Schizonts develop only in capillaries of internal organs
options.feedback: Correct! P. falciparum schizonts develop in the capillaries of internal organs (sequestration).
Incorrect. The above answer is correct.

question: Crescent-shaped or banana-shaped gametocytes are seen in infection with

options.feedback: Incorrect. P. vivax gametocytes are round.
Correct! P. falciparum gametocytes are crescent/banana-shaped.
Incorrect. P. malariae gametocytes are round.
Incorrect. P. ovale gametocytes are round.

question: Malaria is not seen in patients with

options.feedback: Incorrect. G6PD deficiency provides some protection but malaria can still occur.
Incorrect. Sickle cell trait provides protection but malaria can still occur.
Correct! Duffy negative individuals are resistant to P. vivax infection.
Incorrect. Only Duffy negative provides resistance to P. vivax.

question: Which plasmodial infection is more often associated with nephritic syndrome

options.feedback: Incorrect. P. vivax is less commonly associated with nephritic syndrome.
Incorrect. P. falciparum is less commonly associated with nephritic syndrome.
Correct! P. malariae is associated with nephritic syndrome (quartan malaria nephropathy).
Incorrect. P. ovale is rarely associated with nephritic syndrome.

question: Which is the treatment of choice for benign tertian malaria

options.feedback: Incorrect. This is used for other conditions.
Incorrect. Quinine is used for severe malaria.
Incorrect. Mefloquine is used for chloroquine-resistant malaria.
Correct! Chloroquine is the treatment of choice for benign tertian malaria (P. vivax, P. ovale, P. malariae).

question: Gametocidal pernicious malaria may occur in

options.feedback: Incorrect. Pernicious malaria is not associated with P. vivax.
Correct! P. falciparum causes pernicious malaria (severe malaria).
Incorrect. P. malariae does not cause pernicious malaria.
Incorrect. P. ovale does not cause pernicious malaria.

question: Babesiosis is transmitted by

options.feedback: Correct! Babesiosis is transmitted by ticks (Ixodes).
Incorrect. Mites are not vectors for Babesia.
Incorrect. Fleas are not vectors for Babesia.
Incorrect. Mosquitoes are not vectors for Babesia.

question: Maltose cross is a characteristic feature of

options.feedback: Incorrect. Maltose cross is not a feature of Cryptococcus.
Correct! Maltose cross is characteristic of Babesia microti.
Incorrect. Maltose cross is not a feature of Blastomycosis.
Incorrect. Maltose cross is not a feature of Micrococcus.

question: Route of transmission of Toxoplasma

options.feedback: Correct! Toxoplasma can be transmitted through blood (blood transfusion).
Incorrect. While Toxoplasma is shed in cat feces, the primary route of transmission is not feces-to-human.
Incorrect. Urine is not a route of transmission.
Incorrect. Blood is a route of transmission.

question: Toxoplasma gondii lives inside the

options.feedback: Incorrect. T. gondii does not live in the intestinal lumen.
Incorrect. T. gondii does not live in the intestinal lumen.

options.text: Reticuloendothelial cell and many other nucleated cells
options.feedback: Correct! T. gondii is an intracellular parasite that lives in nucleated cells.
Incorrect. T. gondii does not live in RBCs.

question: Oocyst of toxoplasma is found in

options.feedback: Correct! Oocysts are found in cat feces (definitive host).
Incorrect. Dogs are not the definitive host for T. gondii.
Incorrect. Mosquitoes are not involved in T. gondii transmission.
Incorrect. Cows are intermediate hosts.

question: Toxoplasmosis in the fetus can be best confirmed by

options.feedback: Incorrect. Maternal IgM cannot confirm fetal infection.
Correct! Fetal IgM antibodies confirm congenital infection.
Incorrect. Maternal IgG indicates past infection, not fetal infection.
Incorrect. Fetal IgG may be maternal (transferred), so not diagnostic.

question: Intermediate hosts of toxoplasmosis are

options.feedback: Incorrect. Sheep are intermediate hosts, but not the only ones.
Incorrect. Cattle are intermediate hosts, but not the only ones.
Incorrect. Pigs are intermediate hosts, but not the only ones.
Correct! All of these (sheep, cattle, pigs) are intermediate hosts.

question: The following statements regarding congenital toxoplasmosis are correct except

options.text: Most severe form of congenital infection occurs, if it is acquired in 1st trimester
options.feedback: Incorrect. This statement is correct.

options.text: Chorioretinitis and hydrocephalus are common manifestations in congenital infections
options.feedback: Incorrect. This statement is correct.

options.text: Presence of Toxoplasma-specific IgM antibodies in an infant are suggestive of congenital infection
options.feedback: Incorrect. This statement is correct.

options.text: Most severe form of congenital infection occur if it is acquired in 3rd trimester
options.feedback: Correct! This statement is FALSE. The most severe form occurs when acquired in the 1st trimester, not the 3rd.

question: Frenkel's skin test is positive in

options.feedback: Incorrect. Frenkel's test is not for spinal cord compression.
Correct! Frenkel's skin test is used for toxoplasmosis.
Incorrect. Frenkel's test is not for pemphigus.
Incorrect. Frenkel's test is not for pemphigoid.

question: In humans, cryptosporidiosis presents as

options.feedback: Incorrect. Cryptosporidium does not cause meningitis.
Correct! Cryptosporidiosis presents as diarrhea.
Incorrect. Cryptosporidium does not cause pneumonia.
Incorrect. Cryptosporidiosis is usually symptomatic.

question: Which stain demonstrates the oocyst of Cryptosporidium best

options.feedback: Incorrect. HE does not stain Cryptosporidium well.
Incorrect. Gram stain is not used for Cryptosporidium.
Correct! Modified acid-fast stain (Kinyoun) is used to demonstrate Cryptosporidium oocysts.
Incorrect. Modified trichrome is used for Microsporidia.

question: All of the following cause diarrhea except

options.feedback: Incorrect. E. histolytica causes diarrhea.
Incorrect. Giardia causes diarrhea.
Correct! N. fowleri causes meningoencephalitis, NOT diarrhea.
Incorrect. Cyclospora causes diarrhea.

question: The oval oocyst of Isospora belli found in human feces measures

options.feedback: Incorrect. This size is too small.
Incorrect. This size is too small.
Incorrect. This size is too small.
Correct! I. belli oocysts measure 22-33 μm × 10-15 μm.

question: Stool in Isospora belli infection may contain all except

options.feedback: Incorrect. High fecal content is present.
Correct! Blood is NOT typically present in Isospora infection.
Incorrect. Fatty acid crystals are present.
Incorrect. Charcot-Leyden crystals are present.

question: All are true about Microsporidia except

options.text: First protozoan parasite studied by Louis Pasteur
options.feedback: Incorrect. This statement is true - Microsporidia were studied by Pasteur.

options.text: Causative agent of silk worm disease
options.feedback: Incorrect. This statement is true - Microsporidia cause pebrine in silkworms.
Correct! This statement is FALSE. Microsporidia are INTRACELLULAR spore-forming parasites.
Incorrect. This statement is true.

question: Laboratory diagnosis of Microsporidia can be done by all except

options.feedback: Incorrect. Modified trichrome stain is used for diagnosis.
Correct! HE stain is NOT used for Microsporidia diagnosis.
Incorrect. DFA can be used for diagnosis.
Incorrect. EM is used for definitive diagnosis.

question: Enterocytozoon bienneusi preferentially infects

options.feedback: Incorrect. E. bienneusi does not preferentially infect the brain.
Incorrect. E. bienneusi does not preferentially infect the conjunctiva.
Incorrect. E. bienneusi does not preferentially infect the kidneys.
Correct! E. bienneusi preferentially infects the small intestine.

question: Microsporidial keratoconjunctivitis is commonly caused by

options.feedback: Incorrect. E. bienneusi causes intestinal infection.
Incorrect. Vittiforma is not a common cause.
Correct! E. hellem commonly causes keratoconjunctivitis.
Incorrect. E. intestinalis causes intestinal infection.

question: Which of the following has been recently classified as fungus

options.feedback: Correct! P. jirovecii was reclassified as a fungus.
Incorrect. C. parvum remains a protozoan.
Incorrect. I. belli remains a protozoan.
Incorrect. Microsporidia are now classified as fungi.

question: Infective form of Pneumocystis jirovecii is

options.feedback: Incorrect. Tachyzoites are seen in Toxoplasma.
Correct! The cyst is the infective form of P. jirovecii.
Incorrect. Sporozoites are seen in Plasmodium.
Incorrect. The trophozoite is not the infective form.

question: In a case of pneumocystis pneumonia, chest X-ray can reveal all the following except

options.feedback: Correct! Cavitation is NOT seen in PCP (Pneumocystis pneumonia).
Incorrect. Nodular densities can be seen.
Incorrect. Lobar infiltration can be seen.
Incorrect. Honey-comb appearance can be seen.

question: All of the following cause opportunistic infections in AIDS except

options.feedback: Incorrect. P. jirovecii causes opportunistic infections in AIDS.
Incorrect. T. gondii causes opportunistic infections in AIDS.
Correct! E. coli is nonpathogenic and does not cause opportunistic infections in AIDS.
Incorrect. Microsporidia cause opportunistic infections in AIDS.

question: Largest protozoal parasite is

options.feedback: Incorrect. E. histolytica is not the largest.
Incorrect. T. vaginalis is not the largest.
Incorrect. L. donovani is not the largest.
Correct! B. coli is the largest protozoan parasite.

question: The infective form of Balantidium coli is

options.feedback: Incorrect. Tachyzoites are seen in Toxoplasma.
Correct! The cyst is the infective form of B. coli.
Incorrect. Sporozoites are seen in Plasmodium.
Incorrect. Trophozoites are not infective.

question: Which of the following acts as the main reservoir of Balantidium coli infection

options.feedback: Incorrect. Man is not the main reservoir.
Incorrect. Monkeys are not the main reservoir.
Correct! Pigs are the main reservoir of B. coli.
Incorrect. Cows are not the main reservoir.

question: Drug of choice for treating balantidiasis

options.feedback: Incorrect. Doxycycline is not the drug of choice.
Correct! Tetracycline is the drug of choice for balantidiasis.
Incorrect. Metronidazole is not the drug of choice.
Incorrect. Pentamidine is not the drug of choice.

question: Digestive tract is completely absent in

options.feedback: Incorrect. Trematodes have a digestive tract.
Correct! Cestodes (tapeworms) have no digestive tract.
Incorrect. Nematodes have a complete digestive tract.
Incorrect. Only cestodes lack a digestive tract.

question: Sexes are always separate in

options.feedback: Incorrect. Cestodes are hermaphroditic.
Incorrect. Trematodes are hermaphroditic (except schistosomes).
Correct! Nematodes have separate sexes (dioecious).
Incorrect. Nematodes have separate sexes.

question: Nematodes are differentiated from other worms by the following except

options.feedback: Incorrect. Nematodes do not have fragmentation.
Correct! Nematodes are cylindrical, not flat/leaf-like. This is a characteristic of trematodes and cestodes.
Incorrect. Nematodes have separate sexes.
Incorrect. Nematodes have a cylindrical body.

question: Which of the following worm requires two intermediate hosts

options.feedback: Incorrect. T. saginata requires one intermediate host (cattle).
Correct! D. latum requires two intermediate hosts (copepod and fish).
Incorrect. H. nana does not require an intermediate host.
Incorrect. E. granulosus requires one intermediate host.

question: Which of the following statement is true in respect to trematodes

options.feedback: Incorrect. While this is true, it's not the complete answer.
Incorrect. While this is true, it's not the complete answer.
Incorrect. While this is true, it's not the complete answer.
Correct! All three statements are true for trematodes.

question: Autoinfection is a mode of transmission in

options.feedback: Incorrect. Trichinella does not cause autoinfection.
Correct! Cysticercosis can occur through autoinfection (T. solium).
Incorrect. Ancylostoma does not cause autoinfection.
Incorrect. Ascaris does not cause autoinfection.

question: Pigs are reservoir for

options.feedback: Correct! Pigs are the intermediate host/reservoir for T. solium.
Incorrect. Fish are the intermediate host for D. latum.
Incorrect. While pigs can harbor T. spiralis, they are not the primary reservoir for this specific question.
Incorrect. Ancylostoma does not use pigs as a reservoir.

question: On microscopic examination, eggs are seen, but on saturation with salt solution eggs are not seen. The eggs are likely to be of

options.feedback: Incorrect. T. trichiura eggs float in salt solution.
Correct! T. solium eggs do NOT float in saturated salt solution.
Incorrect. Ascaris eggs float in salt solution.
Incorrect. Hookworm eggs float in salt solution.

question: Which of the following is not a cestode

options.feedback: Incorrect. D. latum is a cestode (fish tapeworm).
Incorrect. T. saginata is a cestode (beef tapeworm).
Correct! S. mansoni is a trematode (blood fluke), not a cestode.
Incorrect. E. granulosus is a cestode (hydatid tapeworm).

question: Consumption of uncooked pork is likely to cause which of the following helminthic disease

options.feedback: Incorrect. T. saginata is from beef (cysticercus bovis).
Correct! T. solium is from pork (cysticercus cellulosae).
Incorrect. Hydatid cyst is from E. granulosus (sheep/dogs).
Incorrect. T. trichiura is from soil-contaminated food.

question: All of the following are true about neurocysticercosis, except

options.text: Not acquired by eating contaminated vegetables
options.feedback: Correct! This statement is FALSE. Neurocysticercosis CAN be acquired by eating contaminated vegetables (eggs from fecal-oral route).
Incorrect. This is true - autoinfection can occur through regurgitation.
Incorrect. This is true - eggs are ingested via orofecal route.
Incorrect. This is true - cysticercosis can occur from eating undercooked pork.

question: The longest tapeworm found in man

options.feedback: Correct! D. latum is the longest tapeworm, reaching up to 10 meters.
Incorrect. T. saginata is up to 5-7 meters.
Incorrect. T. solium is up to 3-5 meters.
Incorrect. E. granulosus is only 3-6 mm.

question: Second intermediate host of Diphyllobothrium latum is

options.feedback: Incorrect. Cyclops is the FIRST intermediate host.
Incorrect. Man is the definitive host.
Incorrect. Snails are not involved in D. latum life cycle.
Correct! Freshwater fish is the SECOND intermediate host.

question: Dwarf tapeworm refers to

options.feedback: Incorrect. E. granulosus is the hydatid tapeworm.
Incorrect. Loa loa is a filarial worm.
Correct! H. nana is the dwarf tapeworm.
Incorrect. S. mansoni is a blood fluke.

question: The egg of which of the following parasites consists of polar filaments arising from either end of the embryophore

options.feedback: Incorrect. T. saginata eggs do not have polar filaments.
Incorrect. T. solium eggs do not have polar filaments.
Incorrect. E. granulosus eggs do not have polar filaments.
Correct! H. nana eggs have polar filaments arising from the embryophore.

question: Coenurus is the larval form of

options.feedback: Incorrect. T. solium has cysticercus larva.
Correct! Coenurus is the larval form of T. multiceps.
Incorrect. E. granulosus has hydatid cyst.
Incorrect. E. multilocularis has alveolar hydatid.

question: Larval form of Echinococcus granulosus is seen in

options.feedback: Incorrect. Dogs are the definitive host (adult worm).
Correct! Man is the intermediate host (larval form/hydatid cyst).
Incorrect. Wolves are definitive hosts.
Incorrect. Foxes are definitive hosts.

question: The adult worm of Echinococcus granulosus contains

options.feedback: Correct! E. granulosus has only 3-4 segments (scolex, neck, and 1-2 proglottids).
Incorrect. This is too many for E. granulosus.
Incorrect. This is too many for E. granulosus.
Incorrect. This is too many for E. granulosus.

question: Which skin test is useful for diagnosis of hydatid disease

options.feedback: Correct! Casoni's test is used for hydatid disease.
Incorrect. Schick test is for diphtheria.
Incorrect. Dick's test is for scarlet fever.
Incorrect. Tuberculin test is for tuberculosis.

question: Which of the following flukes is carcinogenic

options.feedback: Incorrect. Fasciola is not carcinogenic.
Correct! Clonorchis sinensis is associated with cholangiocarcinoma.
Incorrect. Paragonimus is not carcinogenic.
Incorrect. Gastrodiscoides is not carcinogenic.

question: Organism causing biliary tract obstruction

options.feedback: Incorrect. Hookworms do not cause biliary obstruction.
Correct! C. sinensis causes biliary tract obstruction.
Incorrect. Strongyloides does not cause biliary obstruction.
Incorrect. Pinworm does not cause biliary obstruction.

question: All float in a saturated salt solution except

options.feedback: Correct! C. sinensis eggs do NOT float in saturated salt solution.
Incorrect. Ascaris eggs float in salt solution.
Incorrect. Strongyloides larvae float in salt solution.
Incorrect. T. trichiura eggs float in salt solution.

question: Terminal spined eggs are seen in

options.feedback: Correct! S. haematobium eggs have a terminal spine.
Incorrect. S. mansoni has a lateral spine.
Incorrect. S. japonicum has a small lateral spine.
Incorrect. C. sinensis eggs have an operculum.

question: Largest trematode infecting humans

options.feedback: Incorrect. F. hepatica is large but not the largest.
Correct! F. buski is the largest trematode infecting humans.
Incorrect. S. haematobium is not the largest.
Incorrect. P. westermani is not the largest.

question: The second intermediate host of Fasciola hepatica is

options.feedback: Incorrect. Snail is the FIRST intermediate host.
Incorrect. F. hepatica does not use fish.
Incorrect. F. hepatica does not use crabs.
Correct! Aquatic plants are the second intermediate host (metacercariae encyst on plants).

question: Schistosoma japonicum resides in

options.feedback: Correct! S. japonicum resides in the superior mesenteric vein.
Incorrect. S. mansoni resides in the inferior mesenteric vein.
Incorrect. The adult worm resides in veins, not the intestinal lumen.
Incorrect. S. japonicum does not reside in the gall bladder.

question: All of the following lead to bloody diarrhea except

options.feedback: Incorrect. S. japonicum can cause bloody diarrhea.
Incorrect. E. histolytica causes bloody diarrhea (amoebic dysentery).
Incorrect. S. mansoni can cause bloody diarrhea.
Correct! S. haematobium causes hematuria, NOT bloody diarrhea.

question: All of the following nematodes are oviparous except

options.feedback: Incorrect. Ascaris is oviparous (lays eggs).
Incorrect. Ancylostoma is oviparous (lays eggs).
Correct! Trichinella is viviparous (produces live larvae).
Incorrect. Enterobius is oviparous (lays eggs).

question: Nematoda residing in large intestine

options.feedback: Incorrect. Necator resides in the small intestine.
Incorrect. Trichinella resides in the small intestine/muscles.
Incorrect. Strongyloides resides in the small intestine.
Correct! Trichuris (whipworm) resides in the large intestine.

question: All of the following are somatic nematodes except

options.feedback: Incorrect. Loa loa is a somatic nematode (tissue parasite).
Correct! C. philippinensis is an intestinal nematode, NOT somatic.
Incorrect. O. volvulus is a somatic nematode (tissue parasite).
Incorrect. B. malayi is a somatic nematode (lymphatic parasite).

question: Most common cause of visceral larva migrans

options.feedback: Incorrect. Ancylostoma can cause cutaneous larva migrans.
Incorrect. Anisakis causes anisakiasis.
Incorrect. Strongyloides causes strongyloidiasis.
Correct! T. canis is the most common cause of visceral larva migrans.

question: Cutaneous larva migrans is due to

options.feedback: Correct! Ancylostoma (hookworm) causes cutaneous larva migrans.
Incorrect. W. bancrofti causes filariasis.
Incorrect. B. malayi causes filariasis.
Incorrect. D. medinensis causes guinea worm disease.

question: A teenager who plays with dogs developed skin rash, eosinophilia, and an enlarged liver and spleen for 1 year. The most likely cause of this infection is

options.feedback: Incorrect. Trichinosis causes muscle pain and eosinophilia.
Incorrect. Schistosomiasis is from water exposure.
Incorrect. Toxoplasmosis is from cat exposure.
Correct! Exposure to dogs (Toxocara canis) causes VLM with hepatosplenomegaly and eosinophilia.

question: Larva found in muscle is

options.feedback: Correct! T. spiralis larvae encyst in muscle.
Incorrect. Hookworm larvae do not encyst in muscle.
Incorrect. Whipworm does not encyst in muscle.
Incorrect. Pinworm does not encyst in muscle.

question: Which of the following is not a neuroparasite

options.feedback: Incorrect. T. solium causes neurocysticercosis.
Incorrect. Acanthamoeba causes granulomatous amoebic encephalitis.
Incorrect. Naegleria causes primary amoebic meningoencephalitis.
Correct! T. spiralis is NOT a neuroparasite; it affects muscle.

question: Which of the following is viviparous

options.feedback: Incorrect. Strongyloides is oviparous (lays eggs that hatch in intestine).
Correct! T. spiralis is viviparous (produces live larvae).
Incorrect. Enterobius is oviparous.
Incorrect. Ascaris is oviparous.

question: Best site for taking biopsy for diagnosis of trichinellosis is

options.feedback: Correct! The deltoid muscle is the best site for biopsy.
Incorrect. While the diaphragm is affected, it's not the biopsy site.
Incorrect. Not the preferred biopsy site.
Incorrect. Liver is not a site for Trichinella larvae.

question: Bachman's test is done to diagnose infections with

options.feedback: Incorrect. Bachman's test is not for Schistosoma.
Correct! Bachman's test is an intradermal test for trichinellosis.
Incorrect. Bachman's test is not for Trichuris.
Incorrect. Bachman's test is not for hookworm.

question: The larval form of Trichinella can be destroyed by

options.feedback: Incorrect. Smoking does not destroy Trichinella larvae.
Correct! Deep freezing destroys Trichinella larvae.
Incorrect. Drying does not destroy Trichinella larvae.
Incorrect. Salting does not destroy Trichinella larvae.

question: Which of the following resides in caecum

options.feedback: Correct! T. trichiura resides in the caecum and large intestine.
Incorrect. Ascaris resides in the small intestine.
Incorrect. Strongyloides resides in the small intestine.
Incorrect. Hookworm resides in the small intestine.

question: The common name for Trichuris is

options.feedback: Incorrect. Roundworm is Ascaris.
Incorrect. Hookworm is Ancylostoma/Necator.
Correct! Trichuris is known as whipworm.
Incorrect. Seaworm is not Trichuris.

question: Heavy infection of Trichuris is characterized by

options.feedback: Incorrect. This is not heavy infection.
Incorrect. This is not the threshold.
Incorrect. This is not the threshold.
Correct! Heavy Trichuris infection is > 50 eggs per stool sample.

question: All of the following statements about Trichuris are correct except

options.text: It resides in large intestine of man
options.feedback: Incorrect. This statement is true.

options.text: Eggs of Trichuris float in saturated salt solution
options.feedback: Incorrect. This statement is true.

options.text: Eggs of Trichuris do not float in saturated salt solution
options.feedback: Correct! This statement is FALSE. T. trichiura eggs DO float in saturated salt solution.

options.text: Embryonated eggs containing rhabditiform larvae are infective to man
options.feedback: Incorrect. This statement is true.

question: Parasites penetrating through skin for entry into the body are

options.feedback: Incorrect. Trichinella enters through ingestion.
Correct! Strongyloides penetrates skin.
Incorrect. Ascaris is ingested.
Incorrect. Trichuris is ingested.

question: Larval form of the following parasites is found in stool except

options.feedback: Incorrect. Rhabditiform larvae are found in stool.
Incorrect. Rhabditiform larvae can be found in stool.
Correct! Ascaris eggs are found in stool, NOT larvae.
Incorrect. Rhabditiform larvae can be found in stool.

question: Autoinfection is seen with

options.feedback: Incorrect. Cryptosporidium does not cause autoinfection.
Correct! Strongyloides stercoralis causes autoinfection.
Incorrect. Giardia does not cause autoinfection.
Incorrect. Gnathostoma does not cause autoinfection.

question: The term larva currens is used for migrating larva of

options.feedback: Correct! Larva currens is the rapidly moving larva of S. stercoralis.
Incorrect. N. americanus causes cutaneous larva migrans.
Incorrect. A. duodenale causes cutaneous larva migrans.
Incorrect. H. nana is a tapeworm.

question: Smallest nematode known to cause infection in man is

options.feedback: Incorrect. T. spiralis is not the smallest.
Correct! S. stercoralis is the smallest nematode.
Incorrect. Hookworms are larger.
Incorrect. Whipworms are larger.

question: Infective form of Strongyloides is

options.feedback: Incorrect. Eggs are not infective.
Incorrect. Rhabditiform larvae are not infective.
Correct! Filariform larvae are the infective form.
Incorrect. Cercaria larvae are seen in trematodes.

question: Baermann's funnel gauze method is used for detection of larva of

options.feedback: Incorrect. Baermann's method is not typically used for Necator.
Correct! Baermann's method is used for Strongyloides larvae.
Incorrect. Baermann's method is not typically used for Ancylostoma.
Incorrect. Baermann's method is not used for Ascaris.

question: Strongyloides can be cultured in/by

options.feedback: Incorrect. NNN medium is for Leishmania.

options.text: Harada Mori method of stool culture
options.feedback: Incorrect. Harada Mori method is for hookworm.
Correct! Agar plate culture is used for Strongyloides.
Incorrect. Hockmeyer's medium is for other parasites.

question: Highest incidence of anemia in the tropics is due to

options.feedback: Correct! Hookworm is the leading cause of anemia in the tropics.
Incorrect. Threadworm does not cause significant anemia.
Incorrect. Ascaris does not cause significant anemia.
Incorrect. Guinea worm does not cause anemia.

question: The average blood loss per worm in ancylostomiasis is

options.feedback: Correct! Each hookworm causes about 0.2 mL blood loss per day.
Incorrect. This is too high.
Incorrect. This is not the correct value.
Incorrect. This is too high.

question: Which of the following does not cause biliary tract obstruction

options.feedback: Incorrect. Ascaris can cause biliary obstruction.
Correct! Hookworm does NOT cause biliary tract obstruction.
Incorrect. C. sinensis causes biliary obstruction.
Incorrect. F. hepatica can cause biliary obstruction.

question: Which of the following stages of Ancylostoma duodenale is infective to human beings

options.feedback: Incorrect. Rhabditiform larvae are not infective.
Correct! Filariform larvae are the infective form.
Incorrect. Eggs are not infective.
Incorrect. Adult worms are not infective.

question: A 6-year-old girl is emaciated with a hemoglobin level of 6 g/dL. Her face appears puffy with swollen eyelids and edema over feet and ankles. There are no laboratory facilities available. The most likely cause of the child's condition is

options.feedback: Incorrect. Schistosomiasis does not typically cause this presentation.
Incorrect. Cercarial dermatitis causes skin rash.
Incorrect. Ascariasis does not cause severe anemia.
Correct! The presentation (severe anemia, edema, malnutrition) is characteristic of hookworm disease.

question: All of the following are characteristics of Ancylostoma except

options.feedback: Incorrect. This statement is true.

options.text: Caudal spine is present in females
options.feedback: Incorrect. This statement is true.

options.text: Head is bent in a direction opposite to body
options.feedback: Correct! This statement is FALSE. In Ancylostoma, the head is bent in the SAME direction as the body.

options.text: Vulval opening is situated in the middle of the body
options.feedback: Incorrect. This statement is true.

question: Most common presenting symptom of thread worm infection amongst the following is

options.feedback: Correct! Abdominal pain is the most common presenting symptom.
Incorrect. Rectal prolapse is not common in threadworm.
Incorrect. Urticaria is not the most common symptom.
Incorrect. Vaginitis is not the most common symptom.

question: Which one of the following does not pass through the lungs

options.feedback: Incorrect. Hookworm larvae pass through the lungs.
Incorrect. Ascaris larvae pass through the lungs.
Incorrect. Strongyloides larvae pass through the lungs.
Correct! Pinworm does NOT pass through the lungs.

question: Infection with which of the following parasites may cause enuresis

options.feedback: Incorrect. Ascaris does not cause enuresis.
Correct! Pinworm infection can cause enuresis (bedwetting).
Incorrect. Trichinella does not cause enuresis.
Incorrect. W. bancrofti does not cause enuresis.

question: History of mild intestinal distress, sleeplessness, itching, and anxiety is seen in preschool child attending play school. Possible parasite agent causing these manifestations is

options.feedback: Incorrect. T. vaginalis causes vaginitis.
Correct! Pinworm infection causes perianal itching, sleeplessness, and anxiety.
Incorrect. Ascaris does not typically cause these symptoms.
Incorrect. Hookworm causes anemia, not these symptoms.

question: The common name for Enterobius vermicularis is

options.feedback: Incorrect. Threadworm is Strongyloides.
Incorrect. Pinworm is one common name.
Correct! Enterobius is known as seatworm (and pinworm).
Incorrect. Whipworm is Trichuris.

question: Which of the following nematodes lays eggs containing larvae

options.feedback: Incorrect. T. spiralis is viviparous (produces larvae).
Correct! Pinworm eggs contain fully developed larvae (infective stage).
Incorrect. B. malayi produces microfilariae.
Incorrect. Ascaris eggs are not infective when laid.

question: Which of the following parasites does not penetrate human skin

options.feedback: Correct! Ascaris does NOT penetrate skin; it is ingested.
Incorrect. Hookworm penetrates skin.
Incorrect. Strongyloides penetrates skin.
Incorrect. Schistosoma cercariae penetrate skin.

question: The common name for Ascaris lumbricoides is

options.feedback: Correct! Ascaris is known as roundworm.
Incorrect. Hookworm is Ancylostoma/Necator.
Incorrect. Threadworm is Strongyloides.
Incorrect. Ascaris is roundworm.

question: The largest intestinal nematode infecting humans is

options.feedback: Incorrect. Hookworm is smaller.
Correct! Ascaris is the largest intestinal nematode.
Incorrect. Pinworm is small.
Incorrect. Ascaris is the largest.

question: All of the following are correct regarding fertilized egg of Ascaris except

options.feedback: Incorrect. This statement is true - fertilized Ascaris eggs are bile-stained.

options.text: Covered by an outer mamillated coat
options.feedback: Incorrect. This statement is true.

options.text: Floats in saturated solution of salt
options.feedback: Incorrect. This statement is true.

options.text: Does not float in saturated solution of salt
options.feedback: Correct! This statement is FALSE. Fertilized Ascaris eggs DO float in saturated salt solution.

question: All of the following parasites have bile-stained eggs except

options.feedback: Incorrect. Ascaris eggs are bile-stained.
Incorrect. Clonorchis eggs are bile-stained.
Incorrect. T. solium eggs are bile-stained.
Correct! Enterobius eggs are NOT bile-stained (they are colorless).

question: Loeffler's syndrome may be seen in infection with

options.feedback: Incorrect. Hookworm can cause Loeffler's syndrome, but it's more commonly associated with Ascaris.
Correct! Loeffler's syndrome (pulmonary infiltrates with eosinophilia) is seen in Ascaris infection.
Incorrect. Trichinella does not cause Loeffler's syndrome.
Incorrect. Trichuris does not cause Loeffler's syndrome.

question: All are true regarding filariasis except

options.feedback: Correct! This statement is FALSE. Man is the DEFINITIVE host (not intermediate).
Incorrect. This statement is true.
Incorrect. This statement is true.
Incorrect. This statement is true.

question: All of the following are true about Brugia malayi except

options.text: The intermediate host in India is Mansonia mosquito
options.feedback: Incorrect. This statement is true.

options.text: The tail tip is free from nuclei
options.feedback: Correct! This statement is FALSE. B. malayi microfilariae have nuclei that extend to the tail tip.

options.text: Nuclei are blurred, so counting is difficult
options.feedback: Incorrect. This statement is true.

options.text: Adult worm is found in the lymphatic system
options.feedback: Incorrect. This statement is true.

question: Hydrocele and edema in foot occurs in

options.feedback: Correct! W. bancrofti causes hydrocele and edema.
Incorrect. B. malayi does not typically cause hydrocele.
Incorrect. B. timori does not typically cause hydrocele.
Incorrect. O. volvulus causes river blindness.

question: In which stage of filariasis are microfilaria seen in peripheral blood

options.feedback: Incorrect. Microfilariae are NOT seen in tropical eosinophilia.
Incorrect. Microfilariae are present in the early stage.
Incorrect. Microfilariae are present in this stage.
Correct! Microfilariae are NOT seen in elephantiasis.

question: Diurnal periodicity is seen in larvae of

options.feedback: Incorrect. B. malayi has nocturnal periodicity.
Incorrect. W. bancrofti has nocturnal periodicity.
Correct! Loa loa has diurnal periodicity (appears in blood during the day).
Incorrect. M. perstans has no periodicity.

question: Which of the following microfilariae is unsheathed

options.feedback: Incorrect. Loa loa microfilariae are sheathed.
Incorrect. W. bancrofti microfilariae are sheathed.
Incorrect. B. malayi microfilariae are sheathed.
Correct! M. perstans microfilariae are unsheathed.

question: All of the following parasites can be detected in urine sample except

options.feedback: Incorrect. W. bancrofti microfilariae can be found in urine (chyluria).
Incorrect. S. haematobium eggs are found in urine.
Incorrect. T. vaginalis can be found in urine.
Correct! Giardia is NOT detected in urine; it is found in stool.

question: Fugitive or calabar swelling is seen in infection with

options.feedback: Incorrect. O. volvulus causes river blindness.
Correct! Calabar swelling is characteristic of Loa loa infection.
Incorrect. W. bancrofti causes lymphatic filariasis.
Incorrect. B. timori causes filariasis.

question: River blindness is the name given to disease caused by

options.feedback: Incorrect. Loa loa causes Calabar swelling.
Correct! O. volvulus causes river blindness (onchocerciasis).
Incorrect. T. gondii causes toxoplasmosis.
Incorrect. Acanthamoeba causes keratitis.

question: The filarial worm which can be seen in conjunctiva is

options.feedback: Incorrect. B. malayi is found in lymphatic system.
Correct! Loa loa can migrate across the conjunctiva.
Incorrect. O. volvulus is found in the eye, not conjunctiva.
Incorrect. Loa loa is correct.

question: Which of the following parasite does not enter into the body by skin penetration

options.feedback: Correct! Dracunculus enters through ingestion of infected copepods.
Incorrect. Hookworm penetrates skin.
Incorrect. Hookworm penetrates skin.
Incorrect. Strongyloides penetrates skin.

question: Definitive host for Guinea worm is

options.feedback: Correct! Man is the definitive host for Dracunculus medinensis.
Incorrect. Cyclops is the intermediate host.
Incorrect. Snails are not involved.
Incorrect. Only man is the definitive host.

question: Guinea worm is

options.feedback: Incorrect. Enterobius is pinworm.
Incorrect. Trichuris is whipworm.
Correct! Guinea worm is Dracunculus medinensis.
Incorrect. T. solium is pork tapeworm.

question: Cyclops is the source of infection in

options.feedback: Incorrect. Cyclops is the intermediate host for Dracunculus.
Incorrect. Cyclops is the intermediate host for Spirometra.
Correct! Cyclops is involved in the life cycle of both Dracunculus and Spirometra.
Incorrect. Cyclops is involved in both.

question: Rat lung worm is the common name of

options.feedback: Incorrect. P. westermani is a lung fluke.
Incorrect. T. canis causes VLM.
Correct! A. cantonensis is the rat lung worm.
Incorrect. M. streptocerca is a filarial worm.

question: Paratenic host for Angiostrongylus cantonensis is

options.feedback: Incorrect. Rat is the definitive host.
Correct! Man is the paratenic (accidental) host for A. cantonensis.
Incorrect. Frogs are not involved in the life cycle.
Incorrect. Camels are not involved.

question: All of the following parasites are found in CSF except

options.feedback: Incorrect. Naegleria is found in CSF.
Incorrect. Acanthamoeba is found in CSF.
Incorrect. Angiostrongylus is found in CSF.
Correct! Trypanosoma is NOT typically found in CSF (it's found in blood).

question: Definitive host for Capillaria philippinensis is

options.feedback: Incorrect. Man is not the definitive host.
Incorrect. Rats are not the definitive host.
Correct! Birds are the definitive host for C. philippinensis.
Incorrect. Fish are the intermediate host.

question: Time of collection of blood is important in

options.feedback: Correct! Blood collection time is important for microfilaria (periodicity).
Incorrect. Trypanosomes do not show periodicity.
Incorrect. Leishmania is not detected in blood.
Incorrect. Babesia does not show periodicity.

question: Modified acid-fast stain is used for the diagnosis of

options.feedback: Incorrect. Modified acid-fast is not used for E. histolytica.
Incorrect. Modified acid-fast is not used for T. gondii.
Correct! Modified acid-fast stain is used for Cryptosporidium oocysts.
Incorrect. Modified acid-fast is not used for L. donovani.

question: Sputum examination is commonly done for detecting the eggs of

options.feedback: Incorrect. Strongyloides larvae are found in stool, not sputum.
Incorrect. E. histolytica is found in stool.
Correct! P. westermani eggs are found in sputum.
Incorrect. Ascaris eggs are found in stool.

question: Larval forms of which parasite can be found in muscle biopsy

options.feedback: Incorrect. Ascaris larvae are not found in muscle.
Correct! Cysticercus larvae of T. solium can be found in muscle.
Incorrect. Trichuris does not encyst in muscle.
Incorrect. Hookworm larvae are not found in muscle.
